checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2014-02-02 end=2015-01-31 value=1059561180.12; NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations start=2014-02-02 end=2015-01-31 value=1774111369.79

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.location: Revenues annual FY2010/FY2011 (revenue declined while inventory rose 44%)

evidence.quote: GrossProfit start=2010-01-31 end=2010-05-01 value=1245136379.76 / Revenues same period value=2082339782.44 (~59.8% margin) vs GrossProfit start=2014-02-02 end=2014-05-03 value=1129227328.93 / Revenues value=2331202044.24 (~48.4% margin)
evidence.location: Quarterly gross margin comparison Q1 FY2010 vs Q1 FY2014

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss Q1 FY2009 (loss quarter, tracks seasonally low revenue)

evidence.quote: Revenues start=2009-02-01 end=2009-05-02 value=1269085335.33 (lowest quarter of the year)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: Allowance for doubtful accounts instant FY2011/FY2012 (-64% decline)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent instant FY2011/FY2012 (-11.4% decline, far smaller than allowance drop)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Revenues series spans start=2007-01-28 through end=2015-05-02 with both annual and quarterly granularity across multiple 10-K/10-Q filings
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent only reported through end=2012-01-28, value=6481307.79, filed 2012-03-27
evidence.location: Allowance series truncated after FY2011, limiting recent-period reserve analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Disproportionate release/reduction of the allowance for doubtful accounts relative to the modest decline in gross receivables, potentially reducing bad debt expense and inflating net receivables and earnings in FY2011 (period ended 2012-01-28).

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2011-01-29 value=17927642.96 dropped to end=2012-01-28 value=6481307.79, a 64% decline
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2011-01-29 value=1118119297.1 vs end=2012-01-28 value=991211737.1, only an 11.4% decline

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Inventory build-up outpacing revenue growth in periods of declining/compressing gross margin (FY2011 and FY2013) suggests possible delayed recognition of markdown/obsolescence reserves, temporarily overstating inventory carrying value and gross profit before later write-downs (timing shift).

rationale_evidence.quote: Gross margin declined from ~59.8% (GrossProfit=1245136379.76 / Revenues=2082339782.44, Q1 FY2010) to ~48.4% (GrossProfit=1129227328.93 / Revenues=2331202044.24, Q1 FY2014)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables grew substantially faster than revenue in FY2009 (receivables +60.6% vs revenue -4.8%) and FY2013 (receivables +37.3% vs revenue +7.4%), consistent with either aggressive revenue recognition, extended customer credit terms, or insufficient reserving against a growing receivables balance not yet reflected in collections.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2009-01-31 value=540557620.07 grew to end=2010-01-30 value=868383287.83 (+60.6%) while Revenues start=2008-02-03 end=2009-01-31 value=7181189247.85 fell to start=2009-02-01 end=2010-01-30 value=6833452360.01 (-4.8%)

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2013-02-02 value=803745446.15 grew to end=2014-02-01 value=1103735320.73 (+37.3%) while Revenues start=2012-01-29 end=2013-02-02 value=7711928769.66 grew to start=2013-02-03 end=2014-02-01 value=8285754506.98 (+7.4%)

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts declined 64% (FY2011->FY2012) far outpacing the 11.4% decline in gross receivables, suggesting a possible reserve release that could have boosted reported earnings

Inventory grew materially faster than revenue in two separate periods (FY2010->FY2011 and FY2012->FY2013) coinciding with a multi-year secular decline in gross margin from ~59.8% to ~48-51%, raising risk of deferred markdown/obsolescence recognition
Receivables grew substantially faster than revenue in FY2009 and FY2013, a pattern often associated with aggressive revenue recognition or under-reserved credit risk

Filing chronology shows a cluster of NT (late filing) notices and amended 10-K/10-Q filings in 2006-2007, indicating a prior history of filing/reporting irregularities for this registrant, though outside the core 2009-2015 financial series window
